exemplar.title: To what extent are current environmental policies effective in reducing the destruction of wildlife habitats and populations in Istanbul, Turkey?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent are current environmental policies effective in reducing the destruction of wildlife habitats and populations in Istanbul, Turkey?

strengths: The political issue (destruction of wildlife habitats in Istanbul) is clearly and explicitly identified
The research question is well formulated, establishing a focused analytical framework
Use of accurate terminology (âthird-generation human rights,â âenvironmental justiceâ) demonstrates solid conceptual framing
weaknesses: Personal justification for choosing the issue is somewhat generic and could be deepened
Connections to course content (sustainable development, economic vs. environmental priorities) are asserted rather than explored in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student excels at explicitly identifying the political issue and framing it with appropriate terminology, the personal justification and linkages to course content lack the richer reflection and depth required for full marks. On balance, the work sits firmly in the mid band (3/4) for this criterion.

strengths: The description of each interview is detailed and precise, noting interlocutor roles, contexts, modes of contact and purposes
The engagement narrative is clearly relevant to the political issue and demonstrates active involvement
Strong contextualization of the engagements ensures relevance and clarity
weaknesses: Explanations of what was learned can be repetitive and occasionally overly descriptive
Connections between specific experiences and theoretical insights lack nuanced reflection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The engagement is described with exemplary clarity and relevance, satisfying the top-band descriptor for this criterion. Although the learning connections are solid, they sometimes lapse into description. Overall, the coherence and specificity of the engagement narrative merit the full 4 marks.

strengths: Analysis effectively compares interview insights with international agreements and secondary sources
Recognition of tensions between economic growth and environmental protection demonstrates critical depth
Good integration of empirical examples (e.g., policy initiatives, grassroots activism) with course concepts
weaknesses: Some key claims (policy enforcement failures, scalability of conservation projects) lack concrete data or citations
Theoretical concepts such as sovereignty and power dynamics are not systematically deployed
Occasional unsupported assertions undermine full justification of all points
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student delivers a thorough, critical analysis that goes well beyond description, applying relevant course concepts and synthesizing multiple sources. Minor gaps in evidence and uneven deployment of theory prevent reaching the absolute top band, but the work clearly exceeds mid-band performance, warranting 5/6.

strengths: Links between interview experiences and theoretical perspectives (international cooperation, environmental justice) are clearly drawn
Multiple perspectives on the issue are acknowledged and briefly evaluated
Conclusions are consistent with the evidence and restate key findings coherently
weaknesses: Synthesis remains somewhat linear rather than fully integrated
Evaluation of counter-arguments lacks systematic weighing of evidence
Conclusions are concise and could be strengthened by addressing limitations or future directions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates solid synthesis by connecting empirical engagements with theoretical frameworks and acknowledging diverse viewpoints. While the treatment does not attain the rich integration or balanced evaluation of the highest band, its overall coherence and consistency justify awarding 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student explicitly states the political issueâdestruction of wildlife habitats in Istanbulâand frames it within a clear research question. Terminology such as âthird-generation human rightsâ and âenvironmental justiceâ shows accurate conceptual framing. The clarity and precision of wording fully meet the descriptor for the top band, justifying the allocation of the highest score within 3-4.

Each of the three interviews is described with names, institutional roles, modes of contact, and purposes. The description is detailed, specific and clearly relevant to the chosen political issue, fully satisfying the top-band descriptor; hence the maximum score of 4.

For every engagement the student outlines what was learned (policy gaps, role of grassroots activism, enforcement weaknesses). While connections between experience and learning are evident, the explanation is sometimes repetitive and descriptive, lacking the nuance needed for the highest mark. A score of 3 captures this solid but not exemplary quality.

The analysis goes beyond description by comparing interview insights with secondary sources and recognising tensions between economic growth and environmental protection. Concepts such as enforcement, governance and international agreements are applied. Nonetheless, the discussion remains surface-level in places and key course concepts (e.g., power, sovereignty) are not systematically deployed, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.

Links to course content are established through references to sustainable development, economic growth versus environmental protection, and human-rights discourse. However, the connections are asserted rather than explored in depth; concepts are not consistently revisited later in the report. This fulfils the descriptor but without the depth meriting a 4, so a score of 3 is awarded.

Several points are supported with interview quotations and external sources, but some important claimsâsuch as the scale of policy enforcement failuresâare asserted without firm evidence. The balance of justified versus unsubstantiated points aligns best with the descriptor âsome of the main points are justified,â warranting a score of 3.

The student recognises divergent perspectives: interviewees prioritise different barriers; counter-arguments stress economic growth. While these viewpoints are acknowledged, evaluation is limited to brief commentary and lacks systematic weighing of evidence, appropriate for a score of 3 within the 3-4 band.

Experiences (interviews) are linked to theoretical ideas like international cooperation and environmental justice, but the treatment is linear rather than fully synthesised. The student moves back and forth between sources instead of weaving them into an integrated argument, satisfying the middle band with a score of 3.

The student explains that the issue matters to them because of a âdeep concern for biodiversityâ and a wish to inspire action. Although personal motivation is evident, the justification is brief and somewhat generic, lacking the richer reflection expected for full marks in the band. Therefore a mid-band score of 3 is most appropriate.

The conclusion restates key findings and is consistent with the body of evidence, but it is concise and does not explore implications or limitations in depth. The work thus fits the descriptor âconclusions are stated, but not entirely balanced or fully developed,â earning 3 marks.

annotations.comment: The student clearly states the issue of human activities destroying wildlife habitats, but the focus on Istanbul could be foregrounded earlier by including the location in the issue statement to tighten the scope from the outset.

The research question is well formulated, clearly linking policy effectiveness with wildlife habitat preservation in Istanbul. This establishes a focused analytical framework aligned with the identified issue.

The introduction outlines multiple drivers of habitat loss but makes broad claims (e.g., regional impact across Turkish provinces) without citations. Strengthen credibility by adding specific data sources to support these assertions.

The paragraph mentions sustainable development and environmental justice briefly; include clear definitions and theoretical context for concepts like âthird-generation human rightsâ to ground the analysis.
This section effectively frames the political issue within global politics, linking environmental justice and human rights. The conceptual framing demonstrates solid connection to course content.

The personal justification expresses genuine concern for biodiversity, but it remains somewhat generic. Deepen reflection by articulating why this issue resonates personally and how it informs your analytical perspective.

The first engagement is described in detail, noting interlocutor roles, institutional context, and learning objectives. This clarity ensures relevance to the political issue and satisfies engagement description criteria.

Interview with the charity president is well contextualized, explaining purpose, expected outcomes, and insight into policy gaps. This detailed account solidly demonstrates the studentâs active engagement.

The narrative integrates description and preliminary analysis without clear separation. For greater clarity, distinguish which observations stem directly from the engagement versus secondary interpretation.

The analysis begins to compare interview insights with international agreements, recognizing enforcement weaknesses. This use of secondary frameworks indicates developing critical analysis of the political issue.
While noting Turkeyâs UNEP commitments, the argument lacks specifics on enforcement failures. Strengthen justification by citing concrete examples or data on non-compliance.
The integration of secondary source critique with interview themes demonstrates emerging synthesis of theory and experience, aligning with analysis of governance and enforcement tensions.

Several claims about policy gaps and EU alignment would benefit from quantitative data or case studies. Including figures on afforestation rates or waste management outcomes would lend weight to these points.
Consider integrating a political concept like sovereignty or power dynamics to deepen analysis of economic growth versus environmental protection, thereby enriching the theoretical underpinning.

Although alternative perspective on economic prioritization is mentioned, the evaluation remains brief. To fulfill multiple perspectives criteria, critically assess the strengths and weaknesses of these counterarguments with supporting evidence.
The account of grassroots activismâs role reflects awareness of multiple actors and power relations. This demonstrates a nuanced understanding of local political dynamics.
Integration of interview insights with theoretical concepts is linear; consider weaving empirical examples into theoretical discussions to create a more integrated argument.
The detailed enumeration of policy initiatives (net-zero, green finance, carbon trading) illustrates comprehensive engagement with policy instruments, enriching the analytical content.

The discussion of successful conservation projects (e.g., European lynx reintroduction) is informative but lacks critical evaluation of scalability or limitations. Reflect on constraints such as funding or stakeholder opposition.
The imperative âdisconnection needs to be destroyedâ uses metaphorical language that undermines academic tone. Rephrase to specify actions for bridging the policyâimplementation gap.
Consider evaluating the effectiveness of local communication strategies by referencing specific case studies or theories on mobilization, rather than general statements.
The reflection on research findings about protected areas and habitat preservation demonstrates strong synthesis of empirical examples and course concepts on environmental governance.
The conclusion restates key findings effectively but does not address limitations or future research directions. Enhance synthesis by acknowledging study constraints and proposing next steps.
The bibliography is comprehensive but uses inconsistent formatting (e.g., punctuation and italics). Adopt a consistent citation style (APA, MLA, etc.) to improve professionalism.

rubricData.name: Global Politics IA: Engagement Activity
rubricData.description: For the new Global Politics syllabus

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
